summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a model rediscovery feature involving Ollama probing, runtime API keys, and dead code cleanup.
summary.outcome: All three P1 review comments were resolved and pushed. A follow-up P2 nit fixing unnecessary write-lock acquisition in `RediscoveryResult::is_empty()` was also fixed and pushed.

repo: Model rediscovery uses a two-step pattern: fetch outside lock (probe_ollama_models_batch_async in fetch_discoverable_models) then register inside lock — this avoids holding the lock during slow I/O.
env_overrides is stored as Arc<RwLock<HashMap>> with an env_overrides_handle() accessor so the gateway can inject UI-stored API keys at runtime (line 1652) before model detection runs.
RediscoveryResult bundles probe results so registration is pure in-memory work inside the lock.

code.finding: RediscoveryResult::is_empty() was checking models.is_empty() rather than whether any models were actually returned, causing unnecessary write-lock acquisition when a provider succeeds but returns 0 models.
Gateway updates env_overrides at line 1652 after loading credential store, ensuring UI-stored API keys are visible during Detect All Models.

workflow: Greptile provided automated PR analysis with confidence scoring (5/5 confidence, safe to merge) that confirmed all P1 concerns were resolved.
PR comments were checked across multiple surfaces: PR-level reviews, issue-level comments, and per-commit comments.

friction: Needed to query PR comments across four separate API calls (reviews, issue comments, commit comments, totals) to confirm no new comments existed.
open_items: The P2 optimization for RediscoveryResult::is_empty() was fixed, but worth verifying the write-lock skip logic behaves correctly for providers that return 0 models after a successful probe.
